name: Literary Fiction
description: Character- and language-forward stories that prioritize psychological depth, thematic complexity, and stylistic craft over plot mechanics. (This graph has 18 nodes — one more than the 17-node standard — because it includes a second Anti-Pattern node to capture an additional genre-specific pitfall.)

nodes.label: Literary Fiction Promise
nodes.definition: The reader is promised a story in which the inner lives of characters, the precision of language, and the weight of ideas carry more authority than plot mechanics â€” a work where meaning accretes slowly and resists easy resolution.
entry_conditions: Story is positioned as literary fiction
exit_conditions: Core constraints are established that fulfill this promise
typical_variants: Psychologically dense character study
Formally experimental narrative
Quiet domestic story with large thematic stakes
Politically or socially engaged literary novel
failure_modes: Promising depth but delivering plotted genre fiction with literary vocabulary
Promising complexity but resolving all ambiguity cleanly at the end
signals_in_text: Prose style is deliberately crafted rather than transparent
Character consciousness receives sustained attention
Thematic questions are posed but not answered didactically

nodes.label: Character Interiority Is the Primary Engine
nodes.definition: The story's momentum comes from what happens inside the character â€” perception, memory, desire, self-deception, and the slow accumulation of understanding â€” rather than from external plot events. External events serve the interior, not the reverse.
exit_conditions: The reader has sustained, privileged access to at least one character's inner life throughout the narrative
typical_variants: First-person confessional
Close third-person with free indirect discourse
Stream of consciousness
Multiple close perspectives
failure_modes: Inner life is summarized rather than rendered
Characters are defined by what they do rather than how they perceive and feel
Interiority is invoked but never actually shown on the page
signals_in_text: Long passages of thought, memory, or perception
External events are filtered through character consciousness
The gap between what a character thinks and what they do is visible and significant

nodes.label: Language and Style Are Expressive Tools
nodes.definition: Prose style is not a neutral delivery mechanism but an active carrier of meaning. Sentence rhythm, word choice, imagery, and structural decisions express character psychology, thematic content, and emotional register â€” the how of telling is inseparable from the what.
exit_conditions: Prose style makes choices that would change the story's meaning if altered â€” it is not interchangeable with plainer language
typical_variants: Lyrical and image-dense prose
Austere and stripped-down minimalism
Formally playful or fragmented structure
Voice-driven narration where idiom is character
failure_modes: Beautiful sentences that do not deepen character or theme
Style as decoration rather than structure
Ornate language concealing absence of insight
signals_in_text: Metaphors that recur and evolve across the text
Sentence structure that enacts emotional states
Prose that cannot be paraphrased without loss

nodes.label: Thematic Complexity Without Easy Answers
nodes.definition: The story engages with ideas â€” about identity, mortality, memory, justice, love, power, or meaning â€” in ways that complicate rather than resolve. The work holds contradictions open rather than collapsing them into a thesis.
exit_conditions: At least one major thematic question is raised and explored through character and event without being resolved into a simple moral
typical_variants: Competing moral frameworks neither of which wins
A character who is right and wrong simultaneously
An ending that changes the meaning of what came before without explaining it
failure_modes: Story has a clear moral that every element illustrates
Characters are spokespeople for positions rather than people
Complexity is gestured at but every real question is answered
signals_in_text: The reader can articulate the story's central question but not the story's answer
Characters who are wrong about themselves in interesting ways
Scenes that can be read as supporting opposite interpretations

nodes.label: Ambiguity Is a Feature, Not a Defect
nodes.definition: The work deliberately withholds certainty â€” about character motivation, event causality, moral judgment, or meaning â€” because ambiguity reflects the actual texture of human experience and invites the reader into active interpretation rather than passive reception.
exit_conditions: At least one significant element of the story remains genuinely open to multiple interpretations, and this openness is purposeful rather than accidental
typical_variants: Unreliable narrator whose account cannot be fully trusted
Ending that refuses resolution
Event that different characters interpret differently with no authoritative answer
Character motivation that is plausible in two incompatible ways
failure_modes: Ambiguity introduced but resolved by the end
Withholding information for suspense rather than meaning
Vagueness confused with ambiguity â€” the story is unclear rather than productively uncertain
signals_in_text: Key events are rendered from angles that prevent certainty
The narrator's reliability is subtly questioned
Readers leave the text still working on it

nodes.label: Psychological Realism
nodes.definition: Literary fiction grounded in the accurate, detailed rendering of how real human minds work â€” the irrationality of desire, the distortions of memory, the persistence of trauma, the gap between self-image and behavior â€” in recognizable social settings.
entry_conditions: Story operates within plausible real-world conditions
exit_conditions: Characters' psychological states are rendered with clinical and emotional precision
Inner life drives plot rather than the reverse
typical_variants: Character study of a self-deceiving protagonist
Trauma narrative rendered from inside the survivor's perception
Portrait of a relationship's interior dynamics
failure_modes: Psychology is named but not shown â€” characters are told to be depressed rather than rendered as depressed
Inner life is consistent and rational rather than contradictory and partial
signals_in_text: Characters believe things that are demonstrably false about themselves
Memory and perception are shown as selective and constructed
Behavior is rooted in prior psychology that has been established

nodes.label: Experimental and Postmodern
nodes.definition: Literary fiction that foregrounds and subverts its own conventions â€” treating form, narrative voice, structure, and the act of storytelling as subjects of inquiry alongside content. The artifice of fiction is visible and intentional.
entry_conditions: Formal experimentation is a primary expressive strategy
exit_conditions: Formal choices are legible as meaningful rather than arbitrary
The work's structure makes an argument that content alone could not
typical_variants: Metafictional narration that addresses the reader or comments on itself
Fragmented or non-linear structure that enacts the theme
Multiple unreliable voices without resolution
Genre pastiche used to defamiliarize familiar material
failure_modes: Experimentation is decorative rather than expressive â€” the story would be identical with conventional form
Difficulty as performance rather than necessity
Form obscures rather than deepens meaning
signals_in_text: Structural patterns that the reader must assemble
Narrative voice that calls attention to its own construction
Gaps and absences that are purposeful and loaded

nodes.label: Domestic Literary Fiction
nodes.definition: Literary fiction set within the texture of ordinary domestic life â€” marriage, family, home, routine â€” where the apparent smallness of the world makes the interior stakes larger by contrast, and where the symbolic weight of everyday objects and rituals carries the theme.
exit_conditions: Domestic details carry symbolic and thematic weight
The ordinary world is rendered with precision that makes it strange and significant
typical_variants: Marriage portrait that reveals what two people cannot say to each other
Parent-child dynamic as site of identity and loss
Home as contested terrain for competing visions of life
failure_modes: Domestic detail is realistic texture without symbolic function
The small world of the story is simply small â€” no magnification occurs
Ordinary life depicted without the precision that makes it resonate
signals_in_text: Recurring domestic objects or rituals that accumulate meaning
Small exchanges that carry disproportionate emotional weight
The familiar made unfamiliar through the precision of the attention paid to it

nodes.label: Social and Political Literary Fiction
nodes.definition: Literary fiction that places individual consciousness in dialogue with systemic forces â€” history, ideology, class, race, power â€” without collapsing the personal into the political or using characters as mere representatives of social positions.
entry_conditions: Social or political systems are active forces shaping character interiority
exit_conditions: The political is felt as personal and the personal reveals the political
Characters are individuated people, not social types
typical_variants: Historical fiction that renders the texture of an era from inside a particular consciousness
Migration or diaspora narrative exploring identity under pressure
Story of a person within a totalized system discovering the limits of that system
failure_modes: Characters are symbols for social positions rather than people
Political argument overwhelms interior life
The social world is backdrop rather than a force that shapes how characters think and feel
signals_in_text: Ideological language infiltrates character's own self-understanding
Systemic forces are felt in the body and the daily life, not only described
The political is revealed through private moments rather than public declarations

nodes.label: Mundane Settings Carry Symbolic Weight
nodes.definition: The literal setting â€” a house, a room, a street, a workplace â€” functions simultaneously as a plausible physical location and as a carrier of symbolic meaning. The work's thematic content is embedded in concrete particulars rather than stated abstractly.
exit_conditions: At least one setting element functions on both literal and symbolic levels, and the symbolic meaning is legible without being stated
typical_variants: A decaying house as emblem of a failing marriage
A garden as site of cultivated versus wild versions of selfhood
A journey through a city as a map of a character's psychological state
failure_modes: Symbolism is announced rather than embedded â€” characters explain what things mean
Physical settings are described accurately but without resonance
Symbolic register is inconsistent â€” some objects carry weight, others are inert
signals_in_text: Descriptions of settings that exceed what narrative economy would require
Objects recur with accumulating significance
The setting changes in ways that track with interior transformation

nodes.label: Time May Be Non-Linear or Subjective
nodes.definition: The story's handling of time is shaped by consciousness rather than chronology. Memory, anticipation, association, and trauma may cause time to fold, compress, expand, or loop â€” because the interior experience of time is not linear, and rendering it truthfully requires non-linear structure.
exit_conditions: The temporal structure of the narrative is motivated by character psychology rather than by plot efficiency
typical_variants: Memory intrusions that disrupt the present-tense narrative
A present moment that is understood only through what the story later reveals about the past
Time collapse in which past and present coexist in a single perception
failure_modes: Non-linearity is decorative â€” the story could be told chronologically without loss
Temporal jumps create confusion rather than meaning
The past is revealed as backstory rather than as living force
signals_in_text: Present-tense scenes interrupted by memories that are rendered with equal vividness
The reader understands an earlier scene differently in light of a later revelation
Time markers are absent or unstable â€” when exactly is 'now' is uncertain

nodes.label: Interior Life Is As Vivid As Exterior Events
nodes.definition: The rendering of a character's inner life â€” their thoughts, feelings, perceptions, associations, and interpretations â€” receives the same quality of attention and narrative space as external events. Interior experience is not compressed or summarized.
exit_conditions: Interior passages are as fully rendered as scene â€” they have texture, duration, and specificity
typical_variants: Long passages of perception during an apparently uneventful moment
A minor exterior event that opens into extensive interior consequence
Scenes where what is happening externally is less important than how the character is registering it
failure_modes: Interior life is reported in summary â€” 'she felt sad' rather than shown
Interiority is limited to moments of crisis and absent from ordinary scenes
Thoughts are logical and articulate rather than associative and partial
signals_in_text: Prose slows down or expands during moments of heightened perception
Sensory detail is filtered through and transformed by character consciousness
Free indirect discourse blurs the boundary between narrator and character voice

nodes.label: Interiority Scene
nodes.definition: At least one extended scene â€” or a pattern of scenes â€” that provides deep, unmediated access to the protagonist's inner life: their self-deceptions, contradictions, desires they cannot articulate, and the texture of how they actually perceive their world.
exit_conditions: The reader knows something true about the character's inner life that the character either does not know or cannot say â€” the gap between their self-understanding and what the text reveals is visible
typical_variants: Stream-of-consciousness passage during an ordinary moment
Memory sequence triggered by a present-tense stimulus
Close observation scene in which the character's perception reveals their psychology
failure_modes: Interiority scene is a monologue of articulate self-analysis rather than rendered consciousness
Character's inner life is identical to their outer presentation â€” no gap, no revelation
Scene ends without having added anything to reader's understanding of the character's inner world
signals_in_text: Syntax and diction shift to track thought rather than action
Associative leaps that the character does not explain
The character notices things that reveal what they are actually thinking about

nodes.label: Epiphany or Shift-in-Understanding Scene
nodes.definition: A scene in which a character arrives at a new understanding â€” of themselves, of another person, of their situation, or of the world â€” that is earned by everything that has come before. The shift is genuine but not necessarily redemptive; it may be a loss of illusion, a recognition of complicity, or the clarification of a grief.
entry_conditions: Sufficient interior life has been established that a shift in understanding is meaningful
The story has built up the conditions for recognition
exit_conditions: The character's perception has changed in a way that is irreversible and that resonates with the story's thematic concerns
typical_variants: Sudden recognition that reorganizes everything previously understood
Gradual, quiet shift that the character can barely name
Negative epiphany â€” a recognition that something hoped for is not true
failure_modes: Epiphany is announced rather than rendered â€” character explains what they have understood
Shift in understanding is not earned by prior material
Epiphany is redemptive and tidy when the story's material called for something more complicated
signals_in_text: A moment in which prose slows and something seen or heard is processed differently
A repetition of an earlier image or phrase that now carries different weight
The character does something small that signals the shift without explaining it

nodes.label: Ambiguous or Resonant Ending Scene
nodes.definition: The final scene or ending withholds narrative closure in favor of resonance â€” leaving the reader with an image, a feeling, or an open question that continues to work on them after the text ends. The story does not resolve its tensions so much as find the right note on which to leave them.
entry_conditions: The story's central tensions have been fully developed
The thematic and emotional material has reached a natural point of rest without requiring resolution
exit_conditions: The ending image or moment is specific and earned, not vague
The ending changes or deepens the meaning of what came before
The reader is left with something to hold â€” an image, a phrase, an unresolved perception
typical_variants: A final image that crystallizes the story's central question
A small action that contains the whole arc
A last sentence that could be read as hopeful or devastating depending on the reader
failure_modes: Ending is deliberately obscure to mimic profundity
All tension is resolved clearly before the ending arrives
The final note is sentimental rather than precise â€” gestures at feeling without locating it
signals_in_text: The last scene is quieter and more condensed than what precedes it
A single concrete detail carries disproportionate weight
The prose in the final passage achieves a different register â€” more open, more still

nodes.label: Contemplative, Precise, Emotionally Layered Tone
nodes.definition: The dominant tone of literary fiction is one of sustained, careful attention â€” to language, to inner life, to the weight of ordinary things. It is not detached or cold; it is emotionally available but controlled, allowing feeling to accumulate through precision rather than through declaration.
exit_conditions: Tone is maintained through prose register, scene pacing, and the quality of attention given to detail
typical_variants: Austere and crystalline â€” every word chosen with visible care
Lyrical and associative â€” language moves by feeling as much as by logic
Ironic and clear-eyed â€” distance that does not preclude feeling
failure_modes: Contemplation slides into inertia â€” nothing is at stake because the prose will not commit
Precision becomes coldness and emotional access is closed off
Emotional layering is replaced by emotional statement â€” the text tells rather than holds
signals_in_text: The prose creates space for the reader to feel rather than instructing them what to feel
Pacing is deliberate â€” scenes have room to breathe
Emotional weight is carried by concrete particulars, not abstract assertions

nodes.label: Beautiful Prose Concealing Empty Story
nodes.definition: Literary fiction fails when stylistic sophistication substitutes for rather than serves psychological depth, thematic seriousness, and human truth. The prose is accomplished but there is no character to care about, no idea being explored, and no felt experience being rendered â€” only the performance of literary ambition.
exit_conditions: Style serves a character, a theme, and an experience that give the language something to carry
typical_variants: Lyrical prose with no psychological subject
Formally innovative work that has renounced content along with convention
Immaculate sentences that describe nothing of consequence
failure_modes: This node IS the failure mode â€” its presence indicates a genre violation
signals_in_text: Sentences can be appreciated in isolation but do not accumulate
No character emerges with interior life despite extensive prose
The reader can admire the writing without being moved by or interested in anything happening in the story

nodes.label: Interiority Subordinated to Plot
nodes.definition: Literary fiction fails when the inner life of characters is treated as connective tissue between plot events â€” when interiority appears only to explain or react to what has happened externally, and the story's engine is event rather than perception.
exit_conditions: Interior life drives the story's momentum and meaning, with plot as the occasion rather than the purpose
typical_variants: Psychologically described characters who nonetheless feel like genre protagonists
Interior monologue that is actually just exposition of what will happen next
Emotional states that exist to motivate the next plot point rather than as experiences in themselves
failure_modes: This node IS the failure mode â€” its presence indicates a genre violation
signals_in_text: Interiority is absent from scenes that are not plot-critical
Characters' inner lives are coherent and purposive rather than contradictory and associative
The story's structure is determined by its events rather than by its consciousness

edges.label: Promise requires character interiority as primary engine
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes are interior â€” understanding, identity, perception â€” not primarily external
edges.effects_on_character: Characters must be rendered from the inside; their consciousness is the narrative's primary subject
common_alternatives: Some literary fiction distributes interiority across an ensemble rather than centering one consciousness
anti_patterns: Interiority treated as backstory that explains plot rather than as the story itself

edges.label: Promise requires language as expressive tool
edges.effects_on_stakes: The how of telling is a form of meaning-making, not merely a delivery mechanism
edges.effects_on_character: Character psychology is expressed through and in language, not only reported by it
common_alternatives: Minimalist literary fiction may pursue expressive plainness rather than lyrical density, but the choice is still purposeful
anti_patterns: Prose style is interchangeable â€” could be replaced by simpler language without loss

edges.label: Promise requires thematic complexity without easy answers
edges.effects_on_stakes: The meaning of events is contested rather than settled
edges.effects_on_character: Characters embody contradictions rather than resolving them
common_alternatives: Some literary fiction explores a single idea with great depth rather than multiple competing ideas
anti_patterns: Every element of the story illustrates the same clear moral position

edges.label: Promise requires productive ambiguity
edges.effects_on_stakes: Certainty is not the promised payoff â€” the reader is invited to work, not to receive answers
edges.effects_on_character: Characters' motivations and inner states may not be fully knowable even to the reader
common_alternatives: Some literary fiction maintains clarity about character and event while sustaining thematic complexity
anti_patterns: Ambiguity introduced early is resolved completely by the ending

edges.label: Promise requires contemplative, layered tone
edges.effects_on_stakes: Tone signals that the work rewards slow, close reading
edges.effects_on_character: Characters' experiences are rendered with precision rather than passion
common_alternatives: Literary fiction may use irony, dark comedy, or fragmentation while maintaining overall tonal seriousness
anti_patterns: Tone is urgent and plot-driven rather than reflective and precise

edges.label: Interiority grounded in realistic psychology
preconditions: Story operates within recognizable real-world conditions
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes are psychological â€” self-knowledge, accurate perception, the cost of self-deception
edges.effects_on_character: Characters have detailed, internally consistent (if contradictory) psychologies rooted in recognizable human experience
common_alternatives: Experimental fiction
Domestic literary fiction
anti_patterns: Psychology is labeled but not rendered â€” characters are described as damaged without the damage being shown

edges.label: Language foregrounded as form becomes experimental
preconditions: Language is a primary expressive tool
Form itself becomes a subject of inquiry
edges.effects_on_stakes: The conventions of storytelling are among the things the story interrogates
edges.effects_on_character: Character is constructed through and by form â€” the shape of the telling is the shape of the consciousness
common_alternatives: Psychological realism
Social literary fiction
anti_patterns: Formal experimentation that is purely aesthetic â€” structure does not carry meaning

edges.label: Interiority situated in domestic life
edges.effects_on_stakes: The ordinary world becomes the site of extraordinary interior experience
edges.effects_on_character: Characters are defined by their relationships to home, family, and routine â€” and by the gap between those roles and their inner selves
common_alternatives: Psychological realism
Social literary fiction
anti_patterns: Domestic setting used as mere backdrop without symbolic or thematic function

edges.label: Thematic complexity engaged through social and political forces
preconditions: Thematic complexity is a core constraint
Social or political systems are active forces in the narrative
edges.effects_on_stakes: Individual stakes are embedded within and shaped by historical and systemic forces
edges.effects_on_character: Character interiority is conditioned by ideology, history, and social position without being reduced to them
common_alternatives: Psychological realism
Domestic literary fiction
anti_patterns: Social forces described but never internalized â€” characters are outside history rather than inside it

edges.label: Psychological realism embeds meaning in the concrete world
edges.effects_on_stakes: The external world is a surface on which the character's psychology is written
edges.effects_on_character: What a character perceives and how they perceive it reveals who they are
common_alternatives: Setting may be realistic without heavy symbolism if psychological rendering carries the full weight
anti_patterns: Settings are described accurately but perceived identically by all characters â€” no filtering through psychology

edges.label: Psychological realism demands equally vivid interior and exterior
edges.effects_on_stakes: Interior events â€” recognitions, denials, shifts in feeling â€” are as consequential as exterior ones
edges.effects_on_character: The rendering of consciousness is as detailed and specific as the rendering of event
common_alternatives: Some psychological realism operates with a cooler distance, but interior access remains privileged
anti_patterns: Interior life is gestured at through summary while exterior events are rendered fully

edges.label: Experimental form frequently disrupts linear time
edges.effects_on_stakes: Temporal structure itself becomes meaningful â€” when something happened is part of the story's meaning
edges.effects_on_character: Character is constructed through the accumulation of temporally displaced fragments rather than linear development
common_alternatives: Some experimental work disrupts space or voice rather than time
anti_patterns: Non-linearity that is decorative â€” the chronological story would be identical

edges.label: Domestic fiction invests mundane detail with symbolic weight
edges.effects_on_stakes: The specific domestic object or routine carries the story's thematic content
edges.effects_on_character: Characters are revealed through their relationship to domestic things â€” how they care for them, neglect them, or transform them
common_alternatives: Domestic detail may operate as precise social observation rather than symbol while still being purposeful
anti_patterns: Domestic objects and routines accumulate without any of them acquiring resonance

edges.label: Social forces are rendered as interior experience
edges.effects_on_stakes: The political becomes personal â€” ideological forces are felt in the body, in daily life, in the texture of thought
edges.effects_on_character: Character's interiority is partially constituted by the social world â€” their self-understanding bears the marks of their historical position
common_alternatives: Some social literary fiction operates at a greater critical distance, but even then the social is filtered through particular consciousness
anti_patterns: Social and political forces are described from outside rather than lived from inside

edges.label: Symbolic setting occasions deep interior rendering
edges.effects_on_stakes: The character's perception of symbolic settings becomes a privileged window into their inner life
edges.effects_on_character: How a character sees the meaningful world around them reveals what they cannot directly say about themselves
common_alternatives: Interiority scenes may also occur in purely neutral settings â€” the trigger is not always symbolic
anti_patterns: Symbolic setting is described but no character perceives or responds to it with interiority

edges.label: Non-linear time creates space for deep interior rendering
preconditions: Non-linear or subjective time is in use
edges.effects_on_stakes: Memory and anticipation are rendered with the same vividness as present experience â€” all are interior events
edges.effects_on_character: The character's relationship to time â€” what they return to, what they cannot escape â€” is part of their characterization
common_alternatives: Linear narrative may also achieve deep interiority through other means
anti_patterns: Non-linear structure in which the temporal jumps are plot-motivated rather than consciousness-motivated

edges.label: Equal interiority and exteriority requires dedicated interior scenes
preconditions: Interior life is rendered as vivid as exterior events
edges.effects_on_stakes: Interior scenes are not interruptions of story but its substance
edges.effects_on_character: The reader's understanding of character comes primarily from interior rendering, not from what characters do
common_alternatives: Some literary fiction achieves interiority through free indirect discourse woven through action scenes rather than through separated interior passages
anti_patterns: Interior life exists only in scenes where a character is physically still â€” missing from scenes of action or dialogue

edges.label: Deep interiority prepares the ground for genuine shift in understanding
preconditions: Sustained interior rendering has established a character's patterns of thought, perception, and self-deception
edges.effects_on_stakes: The epiphany is only meaningful because the reader has seen what it is departing from
edges.effects_on_character: The shift in understanding is rooted in established psychology â€” it is surprising but inevitable in retrospect
common_alternatives: Epiphany may be collective rather than individual in some literary fiction
Some works resist epiphany â€” the refusal to understand is itself the revelation
anti_patterns: Epiphany occurs without the prior interior work that would make it earned

edges.label: Shift in understanding leads to resonant but open ending
preconditions: Epiphany or shift in understanding has occurred
edges.effects_on_stakes: The ending holds the weight of the shift without resolving everything the shift opened up
edges.effects_on_character: Character's new understanding is demonstrated through a final image or action that contains rather than explains what has changed
common_alternatives: Some literary fiction ends before or instead of an epiphany â€” the absence of shift is the ending's meaning
anti_patterns: Epiphany is followed by resolution that closes all ambiguity â€” the ending explains what the epiphany meant

edges.label: Language as primary tool risks becoming language as sole content
preconditions: Language is elevated but not anchored to character, theme, or felt experience
edges.effects_on_stakes: Without substance to carry, style produces admiration without investment
edges.effects_on_character: No character emerges with recognizable interior life despite accomplished prose
anti_patterns: This edge IS the anti-pattern â€” it describes what to avoid

edges.label: Interiority as primary engine fails when plot reasserts primacy
preconditions: Story claims interiority as engine but is actually driven by event
edges.effects_on_stakes: Interior life is instrumentalized â€” it exists to service plot rather than as the story's subject
edges.effects_on_character: Characters become agents of a plot rather than consciousnesses that are the story's reason for being
anti_patterns: This edge IS the anti-pattern â€” it describes what to avoid
